topic: natural cork oxygen transmission and bottle ageing

steps.label: cause
steps.text: A wine suitable for ageing is sealed with natural cork and stored under appropriate stable conditions.

steps.label: mechanism
steps.text: The closure and initial headspace expose the wine to very small amounts of oxygen over time, while the exact transmission rate varies between corks and storage conditions.

steps.label: effect
steps.text: Slow chemical evolution can support integration and tertiary development, but cork does not guarantee improvement and excessive or variable oxygen can cause premature oxidation.

steps.label: exam_formulation
steps.text: Formative causal formulation: limited oxygen exposure can contribute to gradual bottle evolution, with meaningful variation between closures.

_meta.source_note: Inferred formative model - not official WSET assessment or mark-scheme content
